fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but unprofitable, with a high valuation and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — significant future growth expected.
Forward P/E 37.22x — reasonable given high growth estimates.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may be undervalued relative to P/E.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is losing money on operations.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor profitability and asset utilization.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.
Last quarter EPS actual -0.07 vs estimate -0.05 (surprise -44.8%) — earnings miss.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen a dramatic surge, with shares up over 600% in six months, driven by significant transceiver orders and strong analyst growth estimates. While Mark Cuban has urged a focus on foundational AI, the market is weighing whether the substantial upside is already priced in, especially given the company's negative operating margins and recent earnings miss. The stock's high beta and significant run-up suggest volatility is likely to persist.
